ai.summary: Based on the title metadata, this episode addresses how the rapid expansion of AI data centers is straining buildout capacity and available power supply. It frames tensions between accelerating demand for high-density compute and limits in grid, transmission and delivery infrastructure.
bullets: Rapid AI data center growth is testing physical and permitting buildout limits.
Power supply capacity—from generation through transmission and distribution—is a central constraint.

Engage transmission owners and utilities early to align upgrade timelines with data center build schedules.
Stress-test procurement and EPC plans for supply-chain and construction capacity bottlenecks.
